client_profile.identity_model: On-prem Active Directory federated to a cloud IdP; outsourced IT service desk
controls_present: Anomalous MFA enrolment detection on the IdP (alerted to SOC)

incident_sources.label: Cyber Monitoring Centre — systemic event classification (20 Jun 2025)
incident_sources.incentive_bias: independent authority — assesses systemic impact; conservative, lags the event
incident_sources.text: The Cyber Monitoring Centre (CMC) has assessed the cyber events affecting Marks & Spencer and the Co-operative Group during April–May 2025 as a single combined Category 2 systemic cyber event, with an estimated total financial impact in the range of £270m–£440m. The CMC statement addresses the categorisation and aggregate impact of the events; it does not itself set out the technical initial-access method or attribute the attack to a named threat group.

incident_sources.label: Marks & Spencer Group plc — LSE RNS cyber incident updates (22 & 25 Apr 2025)
incident_sources.incentive_bias: victim — frames for liability, understates severity; trust the fact of disclosure not its characterisation
incident_sources.text: Marks & Spencer Group plc confirmed via LSE RNS on 22 April 2025 that it had been managing a cyber incident, that stores remained open and the website and app were operating, that minor temporary changes had been made to store operations, and that it had reported the incident to the relevant data protection supervisory authorities and to the National Cyber Security Centre. A further RNS on 25 April 2025 announced the decision to pause taking orders via its UK and Ireland websites and apps. The disclosures do not state the technical initial-access method, do not name a threat group, and do not describe any credential-database access or impact to virtualisation infrastructure.

incident_sources.label: UK Parliament Business & Trade Committee — Archie Norman (M&S Chairman) oral evidence (8 Jul 2025)
incident_sources.incentive_bias: victim testimony on the parliamentary record — authoritative as to the fact of the vector; may still frame for reputation
incident_sources.text: In oral evidence to the House of Commons Business and Trade Committee on 8 July 2025, M&S Chairman Archie Norman described the entry point as a sophisticated impersonation attack: the attackers socially engineered their way in through a third party that handled IT support, and that third party was induced to reset an internal user's password, giving the attackers a foothold. He confirmed the social-engineering / help-desk reset vector as the initial access method. He did not name the third-party provider in the testimony and described attribution to a specific group as not something the company itself had confirmed.

incident_sources.label: BleepingComputer — M&S breach technical reporting (May 2025)
incident_sources.incentive_bias: specialist press primary — relies on unnamed sources; not corroborated by any IR firm with stated first-hand telemetry
incident_sources.text: Sources familiar with the matter told BleepingComputer that the attackers obtained a copy of the NTDS.dit Active Directory database, giving them access to password hashes for domain accounts, and that the threat actors subsequently used credentials to access and encrypt the company's VMware ESXi virtual machines, causing the operational outages. The reporting links the intrusion to the threat group tracked as Scattered Spider and to the DragonForce ransomware operation. These technical specifics have not been confirmed by the company, a regulator, or an incident-response firm stating first-hand visibility.

incident_sources.label: CISA/FBI Joint Advisory AA23-320A — Scattered Spider TTPs
incident_sources.incentive_bias: actor-pattern intel — NEVER incident-fact; use only to populate inferred/analogous tiers
incident_sources.text: Scattered Spider (UNC3944) is documented to social-engineer IT help desks via voice phishing (T1566.004) and to impersonate employees (T1656) to obtain password and MFA resets. The group's documented post-compromise playbook includes extraction of the NTDS.dit credential database and targeting of VMware ESXi hypervisors for encryption. This advisory describes the actor's general behaviour and is not a statement about any specific named victim.
